orchestkit · #2970 · follow-up to #2948 / #2965

One spawn, copied three times.

#2948 traced 8-10s hook stalls to a synchronous git branch --show-current on the telemetry path. #2965 fixed that one. The filed follow-up said a second copy remained. Investigating it found a third that nobody had mentioned, on a hotter path, and proved the follow-up's proposed fix does not compile.

The three copies

implementationtimeoutcached?pathstatus
env.ts getCachedBranch 5000msper-dir telemetry emit (every event) fixed #2965
session-id-generator.ts:65 2000msenv var cold-cache session id only filed #2970
git.ts:31 getCurrentBranch 5000msNO PreToolUse/Bash — every command unfiled, found here

Measured, not assumed: a fake git shimmed onto PATH counted 1 real spawn per Bash command through the live dispatcher. I had predicted 3; two of the three calling hooks short-circuit. One uncached 5000ms spawn on a path budgeted at 50ms is still the #2948 failure mode, just on a hotter path than the one already fixed.

Why the filed fix doesn't compile

#2970 said: "reuse readBranchFromHead from lib/env.ts"

  ✗ CYCLE
      env.ts  ──imports──▶  session-id-generator.ts   (already, line 14)
         ▲                            │
         └────────── would add ───────┘
      esbuild bundle ⇒ undefined-at-import-time

  ✓ LEAF
                 lib/git-head.ts
                 imports: node:fs, node:path — nothing else
                        ▲        ▲        ▲
                        │        │        │
                   env.ts   session-id   git.ts
                            -generator

      a leaf cannot close a loop. structural, not a rule to remember.
      asserted by a test, so it stays true.

Semantics preserved exactly. The two failure modes stay distinct: '' means repo, detached HEAD and maps to 'detached'; a throw means no readable repo and maps to 'nobranch'. The old spawn split these via empty stdout vs a non-zero exit. Collapsing them would silently change the generated session id.

Coverage

suitecaseswhat it pins
branch-from-head9parser vs a real filesystem (regular repo, worktree gitdir: pointer, detached, malformed, missing) + 2 leaf-property guards
session-id-generator425 spawn-mocking tests rewritten against the leaf, + a detached-HEAD case the old suite could not express
git46cache hit, per-dir isolation, no-spawn

Both "no subprocess" guards match the IMPORT, not the bare word. These files' docstrings name the call they replaced, so a substring match flags its own prose — the same comment-matching false positive that made hq-ext's silent-failure gate unusable. My first draft of the guard hit it.

Out of scope, deliberately: lib/user-identity.ts and lib/sanitize-shell.ts also reference child_process. Neither was measured on a hook hot path, so they stay unswept rather than get changed unverified. · #2970 · #2948
